name: factfulness-ten-reasons-were-wrong-about-the-worldand-why-things-are-better-than-you-think
description: Hans Rosling's Factfulness: Ten Reasons We're Wrong About the World—and Why Things Are Better Than You Think — a critical thinking and data literacy toolkit diagnosing the 10 dramatic instincts that systematically distort our worldview, with practical thinking tools to replace dramatic misconceptions with a fact-based understanding of global progress.
useCase: Replace your dramatic worldview with a fact-based one — learn the 10 instincts that make you wrong about the world and the thinking tools to see reality clearly.
knownRisks: The book's optimism about global trends may feel naive to those who know the data intimately — progress is real but uneven
Rosling's framework risks being used to dismiss legitimate concerns about inequality and injustice
The 'factfulness' mindset can become a form of privilege if it ignores the real suffering that remains
